channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Ubisoft union workers agree settlement, legal advice for game devs, and is Xbox Game Pass too expensive? - Patch Notes #48
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

April 17, 2026

3 Min Read

Logo via Game Developer / Xbox imagery via Microsoft

I schlomped down to London Games Festival (LGF) this week and scarfed down lunch at a cracking little Lebanese takeaway called Bon Appetit. Slap it on your radar if you're planning on attending the event next year. It's just around the corner from the Leonardo Royale—where the Self-Publishing Summit and Games For Change took place—and is out of this world.

My culinary excursion wasn't the only highlight. There was a lot of interesting chatter and takeaways on the show floor, some of which you might have already clocked on this very website. I'll have more to come in the weeks ahead, including tidbits from an interview with Ustwo CEO Maria Sayans. Keep an eye out for that.

This was the first time I've attended the week-long festival, and in all honesty, I was pretty impressed. Now, if only there was something similarly robust in Manchester (or anywhere in the North, for that matter), so I don't need to dredge myself out of bed at the crack of dawn to sit on a ludicrously overpriced train. Somebody make it happen, please! 

Related:Ubisoft Halifax union members agree settlement after studio closure

Ubisoft Halifax union members agree settlement after studio closure

via Game Developer // Union workers at Ubisoft's shuttered Halifax studio have agreed a confidential settlement with the Assassin's Creed maker. The closure came just months after the vast majority of employees at the studio voted to unionize. "We are extremely grateful to our union for negotiating this package for us," said one employee.

Xbox Game Pass ‘has become too expensive,’ says Microsoft’s new gaming chief in leaked memo

via The Verge // Newly-appointed Xbox chief Asha Sharma reportedly feels that Xbox Game Pass is getting a tad pricey and that Microsoft must find a "better value equation." Who could have done this I hear you ask? Well, it has only been a few months since Microsoft raised the price of Xbox Game Pass. Awkward.

Ludum Dare will officially end in October 2028

via Game Developer // What I suspect many people consider to be the OG game jam, Ludum Dare, is slowly winding down. Organiser and co-founder Mike Kasprzak plans to host six more events over the next three years. By the time the final event concludes in October 2028, Ludum Dare will have been running for 25 years. That's some innings.

Epic Games Pins 'Fortnite' Comeback on Disney Partnership

via Bloomberg // Does the House of Mouse hold the key to changing Fortnite's (already pretty lavish) fortunes? Epic seems to think so. The company is purportedly developing a Fortnite-based extraction shooter featuring Disney characters as part of its billion-dollar collaboration with the entertainment giant. I wonder whether Goofy prefers an assault rifle or shotty?

Related:RuneScape developer to expand its IP to the Asia-Pacific region

Roblox is introducing age-based accounts for children and teens

via Game Developer // After being sued multiple times for allegedly placing its youngest users a risk, Roblox has rolled out more new safety features designed to protect children and teens. This week, the company introduced two new age-based accounts—Kids and Select—that will cover used aged between 5 to 15 by limiting access to certain features and experiences.

Among Us has topped 1 billion downloads on Google Play

via Innersloth // One in eight people on planet earth have now downloaded Among Us on Android devices. Either that, or one impossibly dedicated person has been tapping that button like there's no tomorrow. Sus. 

Video game lawyer implores devs to understand ownership and swerve generative AI

via Game Developer // Mishcon de Reya partner Nick Allan shared some quick-fire legal advice with London Games Festival attendees that essentially boiled down to two key points: be incredibly wary of using generative AI and educate yourself on the legality surrounding intellectual property.

Playdate Season 3 is coming in 2026

via Panic // Playdate, the cranky little handheld from Panic, celebrated its fourth birthday this week by confirming another season of ingenious video games is on the way. Season 3 will arrive "in time for the holidays." No word yet on who'll be contributing, but you know it's going to be chock full of bangers. 

Related:US representative Maxwell Frost protests Saudi buyout of EA

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/ubisoft-union-workers-agree-settlement-some-legal-advice-for-game-devs-and-is-xbox-game-pass-too-expensive-
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
